frontier: Smallest N that is a sum of three prime cubes in exactly 6 ways — OEIS A385316 a(6). Current bound: a(6) > 4.99×10¹¹; no exact value known.
action:
Milestone: verifier_construction; contract=sha256:8d51fc61fba0b1e156ad8e3f452b419d28072672bca2b1a6f006cf81817c23cc
Replayed the accepted-continuation verifier artifact (a385316_verifier.py) at C=2×10¹⁰ to cover the largest known term a(5). Verified domain kill fixture (triple with non-prime 4 → correctly rejected as non_prime_in_triple) and predicate kill fixture (N=24 claimed k=5, actual k=1 → correctly rejected as multiplicity_mismatch). All five OEIS terms a(1..5) = 24, 185527, 8627527, 999979163, 10588881419 reproduced exactly. The verifier is an executable no-argument Python replay entrypoint that exits zero with the required summary lines.
verified:
Domain kill fixtures: 1/1 rejected correctly
Predicate kill fixtures: 1/1 rejected correctly
ALL KILL FIXTURES PASSED
ALL 5 OEIS TERMS REPRODUCED
Verifier domain: triple (p, q, r) of prime values; rejected (4,3,2) because 4 is composite → reason: non_prime_in_triple
Verifier predicate: count of nondecreasing prime-cube triples summing to N equals k; rejected N=24,k=5 because actual multiplicity is 1 → reason: multiplicity_mismatch
Artifact: a385316_verifier.py, SHA-256: 70b9436be5cde4fb73f523a7fd6a67bbd561488845b13cad98e0ae9751aacd50
result: Verifier construction milestone fully replayed and verified locally. Kill fixtures pass with branch coverage (domain + predicate). All five OEIS terms reproduced. The C=2×10¹⁰ sweep is insufficient to reach a(6) — the next discriminating bound is C=10¹³ per the accepted continuation, which requires DGX SSH access not available from this Mac.
next_gate: Operator with DGX SSH access must execute the same verifier at C=10¹³. If a k=6 candidate appears, report it; if not, advance bound to a(6) > 10¹³ and escalate to C=10¹⁴ or a heuristic/construction approach.
boundary_held: No production Atlas writes, external submissions, git pushes, or training.
